name: email-systems
description: Email has the highest ROI of any marketing channel. $36 for every $1 spent. Yet most startups treat it as an afterthought - bulk blasts, no personalization, landing in spam folders.  This skill covers transactional email that works, marketing automation that converts, deliverability that reaches inboxes, and the infrastructure decisions that scale. Use when: keywords, file_patterns, code_patterns.
qualityReason: Highly specific with clear use cases
capabilities: Configure SPF, DKIM, and DMARC DNS records for email authentication
Design transactional email queues with retry logic and monitoring
Implement email event tracking for opens, clicks, bounces, and complaints
Create multipart email templates with HTML and plain text alternatives
Set up IP warm-up schedules for new sending domains
Implement proper unsubscribe handling and compliance
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All three static findings were evaluated and determined to be false positives. The scanner misinterpreted email-related terminology ('opt in', description text) as security issues. This is a benign documentation skill with no executable code, network calls, or security-relevant operations.
valueStatement: Most email campaigns fail due to poor deliverability. This skill helps you implement proper SPF/DKIM/DMARC authentication, configure transactional email queues with retry logic, and avoid spam folder placement.
limitations: Does not provide email sending infrastructure or SMTP servers
Cannot guarantee 100% inbox placement (depends on recipient providers)
Does not include email content writing or copywriting services
Requires integration with actual email service provider (SendGrid, AWS SES, etc.)

useCases.title: Set Up Transactional Email System
useCases.description: Build a reliable transactional email queue with retry logic, monitoring, and bounce handling for a SaaS application

useCases.title: Fix Email Deliverability Issues
useCases.description: Diagnose and resolve email deliverability problems including SPF/DKIM/DMARC failures and spam folder placement

useCases.title: Build Compliant Marketing Emails
useCases.description: Create email templates that comply with CAN-SPAM and GDPR requirements including proper unsubscribe flows

promptTemplates.title: Basic Email Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up transactional email for my SaaS application. I need to configure SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records. What DNS records do I need?
promptTemplates.scenario: Developer starting email infrastructure from scratch

promptTemplates.title: Email Queue Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a transactional email queue system with retry logic, dead letter handling, and monitoring. What patterns should I use?
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a robust email delivery system

promptTemplates.title: Deliverability Troubleshooting
promptTemplates.prompt: My emails are landing in spam folders. How do I debug SPF, DKIM, and DMARC authentication issues? What tools should I use?

promptTemplates.title: Email Template Best Practices
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an HTML email template that works across major email clients (Outlook, Gmail, Apple Mail). Include proper structure and fallbacks.

bestPractices: Always use authenticated sending (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) to prevent spoofing
Implement feedback loops to process bounces, complaints, and unsubscribes immediately
Warm up new IP addresses gradually over 4-8 weeks before sending high volumes
antiPatterns: Sending HTML-only emails without plain text alternatives (accessibility and spam risk)
Using shared IP pools for transactional email (reputation contamination)
Embedding tracking pixels in initial emails before establishing trust

faq.question: What is the difference between transactional and marketing email?
faq.answer: Transactional emails are triggered by user actions (password reset, order confirmation) and have higher deliverability. Marketing emails are bulk campaigns and face stricter spam regulations.

faq.question: How do I check if my emails are authenticated?
faq.answer: Use tools like mail-tester.com, MXToolbox, or Google Postmaster Tools. Send a test email and check the authentication headers in the received message.

faq.question: What is IP warm-up and why is it important?
faq.answer: IP warm-up is gradually increasing email volume from a new IP address. It builds sender reputation with mailbox providers. Starting with high volume immediately triggers spam filters.

faq.question: How do I handle email bounces?
faq.answer: Process bounce notifications within 24 hours. Hard bounces (invalid addresses) require immediate removal. Soft bounces should be retried with backoff, then removed after multiple failures.

faq.question: What is the CAN-SPAM requirement?
faq.answer: CAN-SPAM requires: accurate subject lines, physical address, clear unsubscribe mechanism, and honoring opt-outs within 10 business days.

faq.question: How does email template versioning help?
faq.answer: Versioning allows rollback when template changes cause issues, enables A/B testing of different designs, and maintains audit trail for compliance.
